items.name: Anchor Of The Drowned

entries.name: Anchor of the Drowned
entries: The anchor used to drown the bearer, transformed into their weapon.
As the wielders power grows, so too does their weapon, unlocking new abilities upon reaching certain Warlock levels.
This weapon is counted as a magical with a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls starting at level 3, +2 at level 7, and +3 at level 13.

entries.name: Drowning Chain (level 1)
entries: The anchor has a section of the chain still wrapped around it, giving it a reach of 15ft.

entries.name: Abyssal Power (level 3)
entries: This weapon has a number of charges equal to 2 plus your proficiency bonus. It regains 1d4 (changing to 1d6 at lvl 10) charges every day at dusk.

entries.name: Abyssal Aura (level 3)
entries: As an action, spend two charges to cast the Shadow of Moil spell without expending a spell slot.

When this spell is cast using the Anchor, it does not require concentration, deals cold damage instead of necrotic, and allows you to see normally in both magical and nonmagical darkness to a distance of 120ft

entries.name: Vengeance of the Drowned (level 5)
entries: When you hit with this weapon, you may spend a charge to force the target to make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC.
On a fail, the target becomes restrained for 1 minute as skeletal arms reach up to drag it down.
A creature restrained in this way may make a Strength saving throw as an action to attempt to free itself, ending the effect on a success.

entries.name: Drowning curse (level 7)
entries: As an action, you can target 1 creature you can see, and expend any number of charges. That creature must make a CON save using your spell save DC, and on a failed save, takes 1d6 necrotic damage per charge spent, and begins to drown as their lungs full with water.
This effect lasts a number of turns equal to the number of charges spent, and a creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending it's effects on a save.
If a creature succeeds in their initial CON save, they take half damage and do not suffer from the drowning effects.

entries.name: Invocations of the Betrayed (level 9)
entries: When this weapon hits a creature, you can expend 2 charges to activate one of your eldritch invocations that normally trigger from a hit with eldritch blast.

entries.name: The reach of Betrayal (level 11)
entries: As a bonus action, you can spend any number of charges to increase this weapons reach by 5ft for every charge spent.
This effect lasts for 1 minute, and is treated as you concentrating on a spell.

entries.name: Grenade Belt (Prototype)
entries: A reinforced utility belt fitted with six alchemical grenade housings. Through a combination of arcane engineering and volatile reagents, spells can be condensed into throwable devices.

entries.name: Charges
entries: The belt can store up to 6 charges worth of grenades at any one time.
A grenade occupies a number of charges equal to the spell level stored within it, to a maximum of 3 charges per grenade.
The belt cannot store more than 6 charges total.

entries.name: Creating Grenades
entries: During a long rest, or during a short rest in which you do not gain the normal benefits of resting, you may create grenade capsules by expending spell slots and paying the appropriate material cost.

Grenades are highly volatile and can only be stored safely within the Grenade Belt. A grenade removed from the belt immediately becomes unstable and is destroyed at the end of your next turn if not returned to the belt.
To create a grenade:

items: Choose a valid spell.
Expend spell slots whose combined levels equal the desired grenade charge value (maximum 3).
Pay the associated material cost.

entries: A grenade's charge value determines the maximum level of spell that may be stored within it.
For example:

items: A 1-charge grenade may store a 1st-level spell.
A 2-charge grenade may store a 1st- or 2nd-level spell.
A 3-charge grenade may store a spell of up to 3rd level.

entries: A spell is considered to be 'upcast' when stored in a capsule of higher charge level than the spell.
Material costs may be paid using gold or materials of equivalent value.

Wizard spells require additional reagents due to their unfamiliar magical structure. To create a grenade containing a Wizard spell, you must also possess the appropriate spell scroll, which is not consumed during creation.

entries.name: Throwing a Grenade
entries: As an action, you may throw a grenade to a point within 60 feet, provided there is sufficient vertical space to arc the throw. Otherwise, the grenade's range is reduced to 20 feet.
When the grenade lands, the stored spell is immediately released as though you had cast it from that location.
Any attack rolls, saving throws, spell attack modifiers, and spell save DCs use your statistics.
The grenade is destroyed after use.

entries.name: Limitations
entries: A grenade may only store a spell that meets all of the following requirements:

items: The spell is on the Artificer spell list, or is copied from a Wizard spell scroll.
The spell has a casting time of 1 action.
The spell is 3rd level or lower.
The spell does not have a range of Self.
The spell creates an area of effect or targets a point in space.
The spell does not consume costly material components beyond those required to create the grenade.

entries.name: Prototype Bonus
entries: When you first acquire this invention, you gain Wizard spell scrolls with a combined spell level total of 3.

items.name: Pain Link (Prototype)
items.reqAttune: by an artificer with an Iron Defender

entries.name: Pain Link (Prototype)
entries: A pair of humming brass conductors linked by unstable arcane circuitry. One is worn by the artificer, while the other is integrated into their Iron Defender.
The devices attempt to distribute physical trauma between their wearers.

entries.name: Charges
entries: The Pain Link has a number of charges equal to your proficiency bonus.
All expended charges are restored when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Pain Link
entries: When either you or your Iron Defender is hit by an attack, but before damage is rolled, you may expend 1 charge and use your reaction to activate the Pain Link.
The creature that will receive the transferred damage immediately makes a Constitution ability check.
After the check is rolled, consult the table below.
The chosen amount of damage is transferred from the original target to the linked creature.
Transferred damage retains its original type and cannot be reduced, prevented, redirected, or transferred again by the Pain Link.
For example, if Dave is struck for 40 slashing damage and you roll a Constitution check of 16, Dave takes 10 damage and you take 30 damage.

entries.name: Feedback Shock
entries: The link strains both body and machine.
If the Constitution check result is 4 or less:

items: Both linked creatures take 1d6 psychic damage.

entries.name: Arcane Conductors
entries: The Pain Link functions only while:

items: Both creatures are on the same plane of existence.
Both creatures are within 120 feet of one another.
Both creatures are conscious.

entries: If any of these conditions are not met, the link immediately becomes dormant.

entries.name: Experimental Prototype
entries: The Pain Link is considered a prototype invention.
At the DM's discretion, repeated field testing may reveal improvements, modifications, or unexpected side effects.

entries.name: Yoink Box (Prototype)
entries: A whimsical, spring-loaded contraption disguised as a wooden jack-in-the-box. When activated, it violently unfolds into a grasping mechanical arm, snapping through space to retrieve nearby objects.
The device is helpful in principle, and unpredictable in practice.

entries.name: Charges
entries: The apparatus has 3 charges, which are restored when you finish a long rest.
You may expend multiple charges when activating the apparatus to trigger Overclocked Retrieval Effects.

entries.name: Snatch Protocol
entries: When a creature or object within 30 feet that you can see, you may expend 1 charge and use your action or reaction to activate the apparatus.
Choose one target:

items: A non-magical object not being worn or carried.
An object held or carried by a creature.
A potion, vial, or similarly small item carried by a creature.
An object in an unoccupied space or unsecured container.

entries: The target must be Small or smaller.

entries.name: Retrieval Check
entries: If the target is not unattended, make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check contested by the target's Strength (Athletics) check.
On a success, the item is pulled into your hand or deposited in a space within 5 feet of you.
On a failure, the attempt fails and the charge is still expended.

entries.name: Disarm Pulse (2 charges total)
entries: If targeting a creature holding an item:

items: The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = your spell save DC) or drop the item.
The item is immediately retrieved into your hand.
On a success, the normal retrieval check is still made, but the creature has advantage on it.

entries.name: Pocket Extraction (2 charges total)
entries: If targeting a creature carrying multiple items:

items: Make the normal retrieval check.
On a success, instead of choosing a known item, you pull a random non-attuned item from the target's inventory.

entries: The DM determines the item, but it must be something physically carried or worn (no artifacts or plot items unless appropriate).

entries.name: Precision Yank (2 charges total)
entries: You gain advantage on the retrieval check.
If you succeed by 5 or more, you may immediately use your reaction to make a second retrieval attempt on the same target.

entries.name: Panic Snatch (3 charges total)
entries: The apparatus becomes violently overexcited:

items: You automatically succeed on the retrieval check.
The target creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC = your spell save DC) or be pulled 10 feet toward you (if possible).
On a failure by 5 or more, the creature is also knocked prone.

entries.name: Emergency Assistance Mode
entries: If targeting a willing creature or unattended object, no check is required.

items: Cannot retrieve creatures.
Cannot retrieve items in extradimensional spaces.
Cannot retrieve items secured by locks, restraints, or active magical containment (unless the DM allows).
Requires line of effect.
